text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It's Valentine's Day. The students are running
around, excitedly giving out chocolates as
symbols of gratitude and affection.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Oh? So this is where you've been. I'm glad I found
you.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Today is Valentine's Day. So I brought a gift
worthy of the occasion.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
I appreciate how hard you work for my sake.
Would you accept this from me?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Hehe! I'm glad. Please, go ahead...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Which would you like to try first—the sweet one
or the salty one?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Hehehe. ♪ You'll see...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: How did you find the flavor?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Japanese sweets seem surprisingly fitting for
Valentine's Day.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Not only are they filling, but decorating them
with seasonal flowers creates a treat for the eyes
as well.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
And a hint of salt adds a layer of flavor which
sweetness alone cannot provide.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
I always associated Valentine's Day with
chocolate, but now...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: ...Um. Was it to your liking?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Or would it have been better to stick with
tried-and-true chocolate?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Oh—well, I'm glad to hear that!

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
I didn't have the time to make them for anyone
else, but I at least wanted to handmake
something for you...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: I hope that I was able to convey my gratitude. ♪

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
After that, she prepares some slightly bitter tea
to go with the sweets.

text_block_list.name: Monologue
text_block_list.text:
It may have been a bit unconventional, but I got
to enjoy a Japanese-y Valentine's Day with Grass
Wonder.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Um... Did the taste not match your palate,
perhaps?

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
While I understand Japanese confectioneries
aren't typically paired with Valentine's Day...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: I wanted to give you something special.
choice_data_list:
Then I'd better come up with something for you,
too!

Then I'd better come up with something for you,
too!

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text: Trainer...

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Thank you. Those words are enough of a gift on
their own.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
But if you truly intend on gifting me something in
return, please find another kind of Japanese
sweet.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
That way, we can put together something more
elaborate and enjoy it with a cup of tea.

text_block_list.name: Grass Wonder
text_block_list.text:
Another time spent like this would be
wonderful. ♪
